name: Kay Khosrow
description: Kay Khosrow, a legendary king of Iran, is a central figure in Persian mythology, particularly celebrated in Ferdowsi's Shahnameh (The Book of Kings). Born from the union of Siyavash, an Iranian prince, and Farangis, the daughter of Afrasiab, the Turanian king, Khosrow's destiny was fraught with conflict and prophecy. He was raised in secrecy in Turan to protect him from Afrasiab's wrath, who sought to extinguish the line of Siyavash. Upon reaching adulthood, Khosrow led Iranian forces to victory against Turan, avenging his father's death and establishing a golden age of peace and justice. He is renowned for his wisdom, piety, and adherence to Zoroastrian principles. Khosrow's reign is characterized by his efforts to establish law and order, promote religious observance, and expand the Iranian empire. He eventually relinquished his throne and mysteriously disappeared into the mountains, ascending to heaven without experiencing death, marking him as a divinely chosen ruler and a symbol of hope for a future savior. His story embodies themes of revenge, justice, divine favor, and the cyclical nature of good and evil.
subtitle: The Righteous King of Iran
achievements: Establishing Peace and Justice in Iran

keyMyths.title: The Birth of Kay Khosrow
keyMyths.description: Born to Siyavash and Farangis, Kay Khosrow's birth was prophesied to bring both great power and conflict. His upbringing in Turan, away from his Iranian heritage, shaped his destiny as a bridge between two warring nations. The Shahnameh details this birth as a turning point.

keyMyths.title: Kay Khosrow's Ascension
keyMyths.description: Unlike mortal kings, Kay Khosrow did not die. Instead, he relinquished his throne and mysteriously disappeared into the mountains, ascending to heaven with his companions. This event solidified his status as a divinely chosen figure.

keyMyths.title: The Revenge for Siyavash
keyMyths.description: Kay Khosrow's primary motivation was to avenge the unjust death of his father, Siyavash, at the hands of Afrasiab. His campaigns against Turan were driven by this desire for justice and retribution, ultimately leading to Afrasiab's defeat.

sources.author: Ferdowsi
sources.description: The primary source for the legends and history of Kay Khosrow.
sources.text: The primary source for the legends and history of Kay Khosrow.

sources.author: Mary Boyce
sources.work: Zoroastrians: Their Religious Beliefs and Practices
